title: Studies on a new K-symbol analytic functions generated by a modified K-symbol Riemann-Liouville fractional calculus
author: Aldawish, Ibrahim
description: Analytic functions are very helpful in many mathematical and scientific uses, such as complex integration, potential theory, and fluid dynamics, due to their geometric features. Particularly conformal mappings are widely used in physics and engineering because they make it possible to convert complex physical issues into simpler ones with simpler answers. We investigate a novel family of analytic functions in the open unit disk using the K-symbol fractional differential operator type Riemann-Liouville fractional calculus of a complex variable. For the analysis and solution of differential equations containing many fractional orders, it offers a potent mathematical framework. There are ongoing determinations to strengthen the mathematical underpinnings of K-symbol fractional calculus theory and investigate its applications in various fields.вҖўNormalization is presented for the K-symbol fractional differential operator. Geometric properties are offered of the proposed K-symbol fractional differential operator, such as the starlikeness property and hence univalency in the open unit disk.вҖўThe formula of the Alexander integral involving the proposed operator is suggested and studied its geometric properties such as convexity.вҖўExamples are illustrated to fit our pure result. Here, the technique is based on the concepts of geometric function theory in the open unit disk, such as the subordination and Jack lemma.

steps_raw.instruction: 1.K-symbol fractional calculus

steps_raw.title: Method details
steps_raw.instruction: Assume that Оӣn is the class of analytic functions в„ҳ in О”вү”{О¶вҲҲC:|О¶|<1} with в„ҳ(m)(0)=0 for m=0,1,...,n, where в„ҳ(0)(0)=в„ҳ(0). And that Пү(0)(О¶)=Пү(О¶), the set of n-dimensional Schwarz functions is defined, as follows:Sn={Пү:Пүisanalytic,|Пү(О¶)|<1inО”andПү(m)(0)=0form=0,1,...,n}.

steps_raw.title: Method details
steps_raw.instruction: Note that when n=0, we obtain the well-known the set of Schwarz functions. Analytic function ПҒ in О” is in the normalized subclass Оӣ if it admits ПҒ(0)=ПҒвҖІ(0)вҲ’1=0. This class contains all the univalent functions SвҠӮОӣ. The class S involves two geometric functions, the convex functions in the set K, and the starlike functions in the set S*. For О·(О¶) and П‘(О¶), if an analytic function П–(О¶)вҲҲО” occurs and satisfies the conditions П–(0)=0,|П–(О¶)|<1 and О·(О¶)=П‘(П–(О¶) then О·(О¶) is stated to be subordinate to П‘(О¶)(see[10]).

steps_raw.title: Method details
steps_raw.instruction: We request the following preliminary [11]:

steps_raw.title: Method details
steps_raw.instruction: Lemma 1(Jack Lemma) Withv(0)=0, assume thatv(О¶)is analytic inО”. At the pointО¶0вҲҲО”, where|v(О¶)|reaches its maximum value on the circle|О¶|=r,the derivative satisfies the equalityО¶0v(О¶0)вҖІ=в„ҳv(О¶0), whereв„ҳвүҘ1is a real number.3.K-Symbol functional class of analytic functions

steps_raw.title: Method details
steps_raw.instruction: The collection of all functions that are analytic in a particular domain is represented by the symbolO, which stands for the functional class of analytic functions. The specific characteristics of this class are determined by the behavior of the function within the domain and by the characteristics of the domain itself. There are many different functions with smoothness and regularity qualities in the complex plane, and they are all included in the functional class of analytic functions. These functions are crucial to complicated analysis and have a wide range of uses in physics and mathematics. Functions with fractional derivatives of a specific order are within the fractional class of analytic functions. Fractional differential equations or fractional power series expansions can be used to model these functions. In this part, by using the K-symbol Riemann-Liouville fractional derivative DkОі (similarly for CDkОі).

steps_raw.title: Method details
steps_raw.instruction: Proposition 2ForgвҲҲОӣ, whereОӣindicates the class of normalized analytic functions inО”taking the formg(О¶)=О¶+вҲ‘n=2вҲһgnО¶n,О¶вҲҲО”, thenDkОіg(О¶)=(О“(2kвҲ’Оі+1k)(k+1вҲ’Оі)kОівҲ’1вҲ’kk)О¶ОіkDkОіg(О¶)is in Оӣ.

steps_raw.title: Method details
steps_raw.instruction: Proof. The K-symbol fractional derative is given, as follows:DkОіg(О¶)=DkОі(О¶+вҲ‘n=2вҲһgnО¶n)=DkОіО¶k/k+вҲ‘n=2вҲһgnDkОіО¶nk/k=(k+1вҲ’Оі)kОівҲ’1вҲ’kk(О“(2kk)О“(kвҲ’Оі+k+1k))О¶kвҲ’Оіk+вҲ‘n=2вҲһgn(nk+1вҲ’Оі)kОівҲ’1вҲ’kk(О“(nk+kk)О“(nkвҲ’Оі+k+1k))О¶nkвҲ’Оіk=(k+1вҲ’Оі)kОівҲ’1вҲ’kk(О“(2)О“(2kвҲ’Оі+1k))О¶kвҲ’Оіk+вҲ‘n=2вҲһgn(nk+1вҲ’Оі)kОівҲ’1вҲ’kk(О“(n+1)О“((n+1)kвҲ’Оі+1k))О¶nkвҲ’Оіk=((k+1вҲ’Оі)kОівҲ’1вҲ’kkО“(2kвҲ’Оі+1k))О¶kвҲ’Оіk+вҲ‘n=2вҲһgn(nk+1вҲ’Оі)kОівҲ’1вҲ’kk(О“(n+1)О“((n+1)kвҲ’Оі+1k))О¶nkвҲ’Оіk.

steps_raw.title: Method details
steps_raw.instruction: To normalize the above function, we define the K-symbol normalized fractional derivative, as follows:DkОіg(О¶)вү”(О“(2kвҲ’Оі+1k)(k+1вҲ’Оі)kОівҲ’1вҲ’kk)О¶ОіkDkОіg(О¶).

steps_raw.title: Method details
steps_raw.instruction: Thus, we obtain the following K-symbol normalized fractional derivative:(2)DkОіg(О¶)вү”О¶+вҲ‘n=2вҲһgn(О“(2kвҲ’Оі+1k)(k+1вҲ’Оі)kОівҲ’1вҲ’kk)((nk+1вҲ’Оі)kОівҲ’1вҲ’kk)(О“(n+1)О“((n+1)kвҲ’Оі+1k))О¶nвү”О¶+вҲ‘n=2вҲһОҳn(k)gnО¶n,whereОҳn(k)вү”(О“(2kвҲ’Оі+1k)(k+1вҲ’Оі)kОівҲ’1вҲ’kk)((nk+1вҲ’Оі)kОівҲ’1вҲ’kk)(О“(n+1)О“((n+1)kвҲ’Оі+1k)).

steps_raw.title: Method details
steps_raw.instruction: Obviously, D11g(О¶) presents the Salagean's differential operator [12], when Оҳn(1)=n. Our aim is to study the geometric properties of the operator (2) with a connection with the main value k>0.
